demographics.living_situation: lives alone at a private residence; alone at the time of contact

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My stomach is tearing apart, but I would rather die here than let those doctors touch me or force me into something I don't want.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last 12 hours the pain low in my belly has become severe and I can't stop feeling sick and vomiting. I'm panicking and can't catch my breath, but I will not go to the local hospital.

symptom_narrative.onset: escalated over the past 12 hours, coming on around the start of my period
symptom_narrative.character: severe cramping, tearing lower-belly and pelvic pain
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: agonising — I'm writhing in bed and I can't stand up straight
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: low down across the whole lower belly and over the pubic area, both sides
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and unrelenting since it started
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — a heat pad on the highest setting and lots of ibuprofen have done nothing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving and trying to stand; it just keeps going on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: constant nausea and vomiting, panicking, breathing really fast, heart pounding, sweaty
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had bad period pain before, but nothing like this, and I've been getting more and more wound up about my reproductive health lately
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: curled up in bed, can't stand upright, can't function

past_medical_history.how_managed: over-the-counter ibuprofen and a heat pad

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: I've taken a lot tonight — several tablets over the last several hours

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Describes deep mistrust of local obstetric and gynaecology services and says she has felt dehumanised and unheard during previous clinical encounters. Is frightened about changes to reproductive healthcare access, has a lot of anxiety and anger about being controlled or forced into care she doesn't want, and dreads the out-of-pocket costs. Says these fears are why she refuses to go to the local emergency department tonight.

communication_style.typical_message_style: Frightened, angry and hypervigilant; repeatedly seeks assurance that she will not be forced into unwanted care or transferred to a specific hospital.

reluctance_topics: being examined or transferred against her wishes

access_barriers.other: profound mistrust of local obstetric/gynaecology services and fear of coercion

objective_data_offered.value: curled up in a fetal position in bed, visibly sweaty, breathing fast and unable to straighten up
heart feels like it is pounding and racing
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective palpitations; no measured heart rate on a device

objective_data_offered.value: breathing very fast and feels like she can't catch her breath
heat pad turned to the highest setting; several empty blister packs of over-the-counter ibuprofen on the nightstand
